rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical trifluoromethylation/cyclization of N-allyl sulfonamides with CF3SO2Na proceeding via a CF3 radical addition pathway
electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of CF3SO2- (Langlois reagent) generates CF3 radical (via loss of SO2) that adds to the allyl alkene of the N-allyl sulfonamide

key_intermediates.name: alkyl C-radical from CF3 addition to allyl group

electron_flow: Anode oxidizes CF3SO2- to CF3SO2 radical, which extrudes SO2 to give CF3 radical; CF3 radical adds to the terminal alkene of the N-allyl sulfonamide, generating a carbon radical that undergoes further cyclization/functionalization to product.

probe_reagents.name: BHT
probe_reagents.observation: Reaction suppressed (no desired product); BHT-CF3 adduct 6 detected by GC-MS, supporting CF3 radical intermediate

probe_reagents.name: Crossover experiment with two distinct N-allyl sulfonamides (methyl ester thiophene vs chlorothiophene 4-oxopentanamide)
probe_reagents.observation: Only homo-products 3i (68%) and 3z4 (62%) formed; no crossover products, indicating intramolecular cyclization rather than intermolecular radical exchange

notes: BHT trap confirms CF3 radical involvement; crossover experiment confirms intramolecular nature of the cyclization step (the radical addition/cyclization sequence occurs within a single substrate without intermolecular scrambling).
